headline: 28 new cases of pertussis recorded as DOH catches up on vaccination | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA -- The Department of Health (DOH) has recorded 28 new pertussis cases across the country from March 10 to 16, which brings the total to 568 from January 1 to March 16.
Meantime, 40 deaths have been reported from January 1 to March 16
"The total number of cases for the same time period in 2023 was only 26, making this year’s tally to date more than 20 times from that of last year," the agency said in a statement.
 "Of the total pertussis cases thus far recorded, at least six out of ten (356, 62.7 percent) were infants below 6 months of age. Three out of four (270, 75.8 percent ) of these infants were either unvaccinated or had an unknown vaccination history. Of the total number of cases regardless of age, close to seven out of ten (384, 67.6 percent ) were either unvaccinated or had an unknown vaccination history," it added.
CALABARZON, Metro Manila, Western Visayas, MIMAROPA, and Central Visayas are among the regions that have listed the most number of local government units showing case increases.
Health centers to remain open this Holy Week to give pertussis vaccines: DOH
"Metro Manila accounted for 27 percent  (58 cases) of the total confirmed cases among all regions. On a biweekly basis, the number of new Pertussis cases did rise from the weeks of February 4 and 11 (122 cases) to the weeks of February 18 and 25 (149 cases). This has gone down to only 73 new cases in the weeks of March 3 and 10," the DOH said.
"While this may show an improvement, the DOH is cautious in interpreting the trend as the number of cases may still change as there may be late consultations and reports," it added.
The Health Department explained antibiotics are available and effective against pertussis, with a doctor prescribing a course of treatment "that should start as early as possible."
"Depending on the antibiotic used and the age and condition of the patient, treatment may run from 4 to 14 days. It is important to consult a doctor and use antibiotics only as prescribed. Do not self-medicate, and always complete the number of days," the agency added.
Health Secretary Ted Herbosa is optimistic that the country will be able to "fight" the respiratory illness, he said in a statement.
DOH on pertussis outbreaks: No need to worry, but stay vigilant
"The medical field has known pertussis for a long time now. We have antibiotics that can treat it. Vaccines are safe and effective against whooping cough; DOH is redistributing on-hand doses to where they are needed the most. In consultation with President Marcos, I have already ordered to fast-track the arrival of 3 million more doses. Please be assured while also being alert," he said.
The DOH has also met with city health officers of each LGU in Metro Manila to discuss updates on pertussis cases, along with actions taken and assistance needed in the region.
The "implementation of heightened surveillance and more aggressive routine immunization activities" was also tackled in the meeting held Tuesday.
PUBLIC 'ADHERENCE'
Speaking to ABS-CBN News, DOH Assistant Secretary Albert Domingo said the agency, health centers, and citizens are "catching up with vaccination".
There are 64,400 vaccine doses available and distributed to different regions nationwide, while the Health Department expects "3 million more pentavalent doses arriving at the soonest possible time", earlier targeted by June.
DOH: 3 million pertussis vaccine doses arriving 'soonest'
Domingo shared they are not keen on the reimposition of mandatory wearing of face masks or restrictions in gatherings due to pertussis outbreaks in some areas in the country, during the Holy Week, Ramadan, and long weekend.
"We all want to be protected against the disease, we all want to get well. And in public health, the activities of people are best done voluntarily," Domingo said.
'We don't even want to use the word 'compliance' in medicine anymore. Between compliance which means 'Sumunod ka,' versus adherence na, 'Tara, gumawa tayo ng solusyon together,' the latter is preferred because this will actually lead to better health outcomes," he added.
RELATED VIDEO:

headline: 3 in 4 Filipinos oppose charter change: Pulse Asia | ABS-CBN News
text:
A multi-sectoral alliance of clergy folk, studentry, indigenous people, workers, and transport groups hold a peaceful demonstration against Charter Change, PGH Oblation Plaza, along Taft Ave. in Manila on February 23, 2024. Jonathan Cellona, ABS-CBN news 
MANILA (UPDATED) -- About three-fourths of Filipinos oppose charter change, according to a Pulse Asia survey released Wednesday.
Of 1,200 adults polled in early March, 74 percent said the constitution "should not be amended now or any other time". 
"This opinion is echoed by small to big majorities in the various areas and classes (69% to 82% and 58% to 80%, respectively)," Pulse Asia said.
It noted that opposition to charter change at the national level went up by 43 percentage points in March 2024 from the same month last year.
Meanwhile, 8 percent of respondents in the latest survey said the 1987 Constitution should be amended now. 
Another 8 percent are open to charter change under the next administration.
Six percent oppose constitutional amendments now but support it at some other time under the incumbent administration.
The remaining 4 percent are undecided on the matter. 
The same poll found that most Filipino adults are not in favor of 10 proposed amendments to the 1987 Constitutions, including the following:
- allowing foreigners to own schools or universities (68 percent)
- lifting the prohibition on foreign ownership of communications (71 percent)
- changing the presidential system of government to a parliamentary one (71 percent)
- allowing foreign individuals and companies to have foreign equity in mass media and advertising (71 percent) 
- changing the present unitary system of government to a federal system (71 percent)
- extending the terms of office for national and local elective officials (73 percent)
- shifting from a bicameral to a unicameral legislature (74 percent) 
- removing limits on shares of stocks in Philippine corporations that may be owned by foreign individuals and companies 
(78 percent)
- allowing foreign individuals and companies to own residential and industrial lands (81 percent)
- allowing foreign individuals and companies to utilize Philippine natural resources (86 percent)
Pulse Asia added that almost half of Filipino adults or 48 percent know little about the constitution.
"The amount of knowledge about the country’s charter is essentially unchanged between March 2023 and March 2024," it said.

President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. maintains he is primarily seeking to amend the charter's economic provisions to allow more foreign investment, and ideally create more jobs.
He has said the political aspects, including term limits, should be tackled later.
Critics warn the effort could lead to the abolishment of term limits, with presidents currently allowed just one six-year stint in office.
Charter change being used for term extension, Duterte warns
The House of Representatives this month approved its version of economic charter change, the Resolution of Both Houses (RBH) No. 7.
The Senate's version of the resolution faces an "uphill climb" to get 18 votes and pass in the chamber, a lawmaker recently said.
House lawmakers who voted against economic charter change urged the government to drop its push for revisions to the 1987 Constitution after the latest Pulse Asia poll. 
"Dapat itigil na ang charter change at ituon ng gobyerno ang oras at resouces nito sa pressing problems ng mga Filipino," House Deputy Minority Leader France Castro said in a statement. 
(Government should stop its charter change push and instead pour its time and resources to the pressing problems of Filipinos.)
 "Pinapatunayan din nitong survey na hindi naman talaga nakikita ng mga kababayan natin na Chacha ang solusyon sa mataas na presyo ng mga bilihin, mababang sahod at kahirapan sa bansa," Kabataan party-list Rep. Raoul Manuel said. 
(This survey also proves that our fellow Filipinos do not see Chach as the solution to the high prices of goods, low salary, and poverty in the country.)
Meanwhile, Senate President Juan Miguel Zubiri said the survey result is a “true eye opener.”
According to Zubiri, the poll firm's reflection of the people's stand on the charter change issue is the main reason why senators are carefully discussing Resolution of Both Houses number 6, which targets to amend some economic provisions in the 1987 Constitution.
"That’s why the Senate will still conduct hearings in Luzon, Visayas at Mindanao to truly see the Pulse of the people when it comes to amending the Constitution and what amendments should be done if ever," Zubiri said in a text statement.
He added: "This survey shows it is an unpopular move with almost 9 out of 10 Filipinos opposed to changing the Charter."
-- With reports from Agence France-Presse, Sherrie Ann Torres, and RG Cruz, ABS-CBN News

headline: 'It is not VP's job to demonize China': Paolo Duterte | ABS-CBN News
text:
Vice President and Education Secretary Sara Duterte delivers a speech during the   launching of the Department of Education’s (DepEd) School-Based Feeding Program (SBFP) and School Mental Health Program at the Esteban Abada Elementary School in Quezon City on Wednesday, January 24, 2024. Maria Tan, ABS-CBN News 
The brother of Vice President and Education Sara Duterte on Wednesday slammed Akbayan party-list for criticizing her for her supposed silence on the escalating tension in the West Philippine Sea.
Davao City First District Rep. Paolo Duterte said questions on the actions of Chinese vessels in the waterway should be directed to the architects of foreign policy, referring to the Secretary of National Defense and the Secretary of Foreign Affairs.
"It is not the job of the Vice President or the Secretary of the Department of Education to demonize China or any country for that matter. We can see this task is your assignment," Duterte said in a statement.
Aside from Vice President Duterte's silence on the sea row, Akbayan party president Rafaela David on Tuesday also scored her defense of televangelist Apollo Quiboloy, who faces sexual abuse and human trafficking accusations.
"It's bewildering how Vice President Duterte can find the time and energy to defend a disgraced figure like Quiboloy, yet remains eerily silent when our own countrymen and women are under attack by a foreign aggressor. Is her allegiance to justice selective, or does she simply lack the courage to stand up to China? Either way, it is despicable," David said.
"Talaga bang kontrabida na ang napiling papel ng mga Duterte sa ating bansa? Sadya bang 'made in China' ang puso ni VP Sara? Kaya niyang ipagtanggol ang isang akusadong rapist at human trafficker, ngunit pipi, bulag at bingi siya sa pang-aapi na dinadanas ng ating mga kababayan sa kamay ng Tsina sa West Philippine Sea," she added.
NSC official dares Roque to explain alleged deal between Duterte admin, China
Vice President Duterte has not issued a statement yet on Akbayan's remarks. However, her brother said critics should also reflect this Holy Week on their remarks against the Vice President in various issues.
"As we pause and pray this Holy Week and reflect on the savior Jesus Christ as he is nailed to the cross, we shall also reflect on why you nail everything to the Vice President. And we fervently hope that your words are not part of the reason why there is action in the WPS," Paolo Duterte said.
"Let us all pray for a peaceful Holy Week for our beloved country that is not disrupted by press propaganda of Akbayan," he added.

headline: 18 Filipinos from tanker seized by Iran back in the Philippines | ABS-CBN News
text:
A handout photo made available by the Islamic Republic of Iran Army office on November 27, 2023 shows soldiers standing guard as they attend the inauguration ceremony of an Iranian warship. Handout / Iranian Army office / AFP 
MANILA — All 18 Filipino seafarers from the tanker MV St. Nikolas held in Iran have safely returned to the Philippines, according to the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA).
“They were repatriated in batches, including the final group of 6 seafarers who arrived in Manila last week,” the DFA said in a statement.
READ: PH sends delegation to Iran to secure Filipino seafarers' freedom  
The Iranian Navy seized MV St. Nikolas in January in retaliation for the US seizure of its crude on the same vessel back in 2023. 
Foreign Affairs Undersecretary Eduardo de Vega previously said that Iran agreed to allow the swap of crew members, but that the DFA demanded from the ship owner and manning agency that no new Filipino crew will be deployed to the ship.
“The Philippine government thanks the authorities of Iran for their understanding in this matter, and appreciates the work of the manning agency which made this possible,” the DFA statement continued.
Only 17 other Filipino seafarers, onboard the cargo carrier Galaxy Leader, are still being held hostage in Yemen by the Houthis. The DFA said it will continue to exhaust all diplomatic channels for their release.
READ: DFA urges Iran to ensure safety of Filipinos in seized oil tanker  
“We continue to work on the safe return to the Philippines of  17 other Filipino seafarers who are still aboard their vessel in the area,” the DFA said.
'WAR-LIKE ZONE' DESIGNATION
The Department of Migrant Workers (DMW) meanwhile lauded the International Bargaining Forum’s (IBF) designation of the southern Red Sea and the Gulf of Aden as a “war-like zone” for seafarers.
“The DMW strongly supports the IBF designation of the southern Red Sea and the Gulf of Aden as a ‘war-like zone. This critical decision recognizes the grave dangers faced by our Filipino seafarers and other nationalities traversing these high-risk sea lanes,” DMW OIC Hans Cacdac said in a statement.
“The ‘war-like zone' designation underscores the need for intensified measures to protect seafarers... by compelling shipping agencies and principals to undertake stricter safety measures," he added.
The designation also highlights a seafarer’s “right to refuse sailing” upon knowing that he or she shall board a ship that will navigate the Red Sea or the Gulf of Aden.
The DMW and Maritime Industry Tripartite Council (MITC) on March 19 called for such designation.
That call came in the wake of continuing missile and drone attacks launched by Yemen-based Houthi rebels on international shipping passing through the crucial sea routes.
READ: US leads strikes vs Iran-backed Houthis

headline: ​5 House bills seek to weed out 'placeholder' candidates during election | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA — Less than a year before aspiring public officials file their candidacies for the 2025 polls, several lawmakers have filed bills seeking to prohibit substitution due to the withdrawal of the original candidate.
Nuisance? Election lawyer weighs in on #Halalan2022 'placeholders'
There are currently 5 bills pending before the House of Representatives seeking to address the supposed loophole in the election process:
• House Bill 10186 filed by San Jose del Monte Bulacan Rep. Florida Robes 
• House Bill 437 by Surigao del Sur 2nd District Rep. Johnny Pimentel 
• House Bill 5692 filed by Cibac Party List Rep. Bro. Eddie Villanueva 
• House Bill 8794 by Quezon City 6th District Rep. Ma. Victoria Co-Pilar
• House Bill 9069 filed by Davao de Oro 2nd District Rep. Ruwel Peter Gonzaga
All the bills seek to delete the ground of "withdrawal by a candidate" from the list of grounds for substitution.
Without mentioning any names, Pimentel said this ground of withdrawal has been abused by persons who serve as placeholders.
"This has allowed political parties, both national and local, to field non-bona fide candidates merely to comply with the electoral deadline of filing for candidacy in mockery of our electoral system," Pimentel said in his explanatory note.
His bill would consider place holders as nuisance candidates while making being a nuisance candidate an election offense.
Admission of being placeholder candidate not enough to be nuisance: Comelec spox
Villanueva cited similar reasons.
"Since the withdrawal need not be justified for the substitution to be accepted by the Commission on Elections, it is easy to field any person as a candidate to beat the Comelec deadline and just substitute another after the cutoff date," Villanueva said in his explanatory note to his bill, which also adds incapacity as a ground for substitution.
Like Villanueva's bill, Co-Pilar's bill also removed withdrawal as a ground while adding permanent incapacity as a ground.
"The approval of this bill will prevent any political party in misleading the Filipino voters," Gonzaga said in his explanatory note to his own bill.
"If approved, this amendment would drastically change how elections are conducted in the Philippines. By limiting political parties' ability to substitute candidates in cases of voluntary withdrawal, it would guarantee that the candidates who are put in front of the public during the campaign will be the ones who, should they be elected, serve the people," Robes said in her own bill.
The most famous case of candidate substitution was when former President Rodrigo Duterte substituted as a candidate for President in 2016.
His own daughter, now Vice President Sara Duterte, was also a substitute candidate in 2022.
EXCLUSIVE: Lakas-CMD official admits filers for president, VP just 'placeholders'
In both cases, the previous candidate withdrew from the race before the substitution deadline.

headline: Roque asked to explain alleged ‘status quo agreement’ between Duterte administration, China | ABS-CBN News
text: Philippine security officials seek more details of a supposed deal by then-president Rodrigo Duterte with China. Duterte’s former spokesperson claims Manila’s violation of this agreement provoked China’s violent actions in the West Philippine Sea. — The World Tonight, ANC, March 27, 2024

headline: Filipino marine biologists find signs of overfishing, pollution in Pag-Asa Island sandy cays | ABS-CBN News
text: Philippine marine biologists discover the sorry state of sandy cays in Pag-Asa Island due to overfishing and pollution caused by the permanent presence of Chinese ships in those areas. Their research activities, however, was cut short as Chinese interference posed a danger to the group. — The World Tonight, ANC, March 27, 2024

headline: Fil-Am migrant nonprofit finds home in Long Beach | ABS-CBN News
text:
A Fil-Am nonprofit focusing on the welfare of Filipino workers and immigrants has found a permanent home in Long Beach, California. 
The Filipino Migrant Center – after 14 years of service – opened its new headquarters in a neighborhood with one of the highest concentrations of Filipinos in the city. 
“This is super important for us to have this space,” said FMC executive director Romeo Hebron. “This is really the heart of the Filipino community. For us to have this stand alone location, it’s been many years in the making.”
Throughout its history, the FMC has been at the forefront of combatting wage theft, human trafficking, and anti-Asian hate. 
In the past two years, it led the charge in anti-Asian hate issues, and rallied around hate crime victims like the Roque family, who were at the opening ceremony for the FMC’s office. 
The FMC expressed hope that the space will provide a place to tackle issues facing the community. 
“We have a lot of ongoing campaigns,” Romeo said, “to end wage theft in the workplace, to demand justice and accountability for victims and survivors of Asian hate.”
He added: “As we continue with those campaigns, we now have this place here in west Long Beach. We know we’ll have the capacity to carry on any new campaigns that might develop, whether it’s around workers’ rights, immigrant rights, or health and wellness.”
At core of the non-profit’s advocacies is the wellbeing of migrant workers.
Lester Ramos is one of the human trafficking victims assisted by the FMC. After a decade, he has become a workers’ leader and now uses his experiences to aid fellow Filipinos. 
“We help address a lot of issues in the FMC,” said Ramos in Filipino. “A lot of caregivers cannot receive their payment on time. There’s no overtime, [there’s] a lot of exploitation, and then employers take advantage of their wages and we have Asian hate existing.”
Aside from pressing issues, the new FMC center also looks to be a cultural hub where locals can find fellowship among Filipinos. 
With activities ranging from cultural programs for the youth, to a walking club for the seniors – the center hopes to be a unifying landmark for Filipinos in the area.

headline: Fil-Am NY state assemblyman to run for reelection | ABS-CBN News
text:
Filipino-American Steven Raga is running for a second term as state assemblyman in New York. 
The first Fil-Am to have won an elected post in New York in 2022 described his first term as highly successful.
Fil-Am community lauds Steven Raga's historic election win in NY
“We've had for the first time, investment in our community,” said Raga. “We've had a collaboration with other elected officials who want to support the FilAm community and we have a broad coalition of folks that want to invest, look at, and make sure our district is taken care of.”
The National Federation of Filipino-American Associations New York collaborated with Raga and other officials to ensure federal government investment for the Fil-Am community. 
The result was a $1.6 million grant to help build a Filipino community center in Queens. 
“This is just the first step,” said NAFFAA New York president Rachelle Ocampo. “It's just the beginning.”
The NAFFAA said the federal grant would not be possible without the involvement of elected officials in New York. 
“I think a lot of that really comes down to the fact that we have Steve,” said Michelle Amor, NAFFAA executive director. “He was the one who was able to help us create a relationship with congress members and in turn, our relations with President Biden.”
Earlier this month, Raga also introduced a bill that seeks more transparency at the local government level.
His proposal aims to streamline requests for Freedom of Information law.
These often take weeks, months or even years. But under the bill, the government would be mandated to acknowledge the requests promptly, and provide a realistic timeline for approval or denial.
The move is seen to facilitate easier access to public records not just for journalists but also for average New Yorkers. 
“[It] should be open to everybody and my bill makes sure you get a response in 30 days,” Raga said.
Members of the Fil-Am community also came to show their support for Raga.
“It’s a rising tide [that] lifts all boats,” said Charina Amunategui. “For us to support Steven, keep him in office, it lifts the profiles of all Filipinos, for the world to see our brand of hard work, of integrity, of tirelessly serving our community.”
The New York state assembly primary is happening on June 25, while the general election is set for November 5.

headline: Palm Sunday prayer rally held in BC vs. PH charter change | ABS-CBN News
text:
Young Filipino-Canadian activists in Vancouver condemned the passage of a House resolution in the Philippines to amend the 1987 Constitution.
The activist groups staged a prayer-protest rally against charter change across the street from St. Mary’s Church in Vancouver on Palm Sunday.
They expressed hope that Filipino Catholics going to church will hear about the proposed charter amendments being pushed by allies of the current administration.
“Who will benefit from that? Not the Filipino people,” said Jaela Villegas, vice chairperson of BAYAN Canada. 
Gabriela B.C. strongly opposed the proposed amendment that will allow more foreign ownership in key industries.
The group fears this could create more economic hardship for Filipinos, which could lead to more cases of violence against women.
“Poverty correlates to violence for Filipino women,” said Mackie Cairns. “We saw that in COVID-19 when, [with] inflation and people were locked down, there was an increased amount of violence and cases even against violence against children.”
Meanwhile, student members of the Sulong UBC, Bayan Canada, and Migrante BC, distributed leaflets about the proposed charter change to parishioners of St. Mary's church.
Some Filipino churchgoers said they are in favor of charter change, while others thumbed down on the move. 
“I am in favor as long as our president will do it for our good, for the Philippines to be progressive,” said Omar Geronimo in Filipino.
Said Pristine Golez, another parishioner: “Our resources should be reserved for the Filipinos. So, if they’re extending that to the ownership of the foreigners, what will happen to the Filipinos?”
Villegas says their anti-charter change protest was timed at the start of the Holy Week so that Filipinos may be encouraged to follow the example of Jesus Christ who loved the poor. 
“This Holy Week, [let’s remember] that as Filipinos, we should be like Christians,” Villegas said. “”Good Christians should be like Jesus who [fought] for the rights, human rights of people and everybody else."

headline: Maria Gigante tinanghal na Universal Woman 2024 | ABS-CBN News
text:
CAMBODIA - Nagbunyi ang Pilipinas sa pagkapanalo ni Maria Gigante na kinoronahang 2024 Universal Woman. Matagal man ang hinintay para maabot ang pangarap, naniniwala si Maria na ito ang perfect timing ng Diyos para sa kanya.
 
“To me, Universal Woman means the future. I learned from working with this organization that I want to live in a world that is filled with respect and collaboration. One that encourages us to step into our differences instead of ignoring them. And learn to harness them in order affect positive social change. 
 
I want to be in this organization because I feel that I am not alone. I am one in an army of women who are ready and willing to make a difference. To support a world that is not only sustainable, but that stands for economic progress, and positive, inclusive, social impact. If you are here tonight, welcome to the new era of beauty,” pahayag ni Gigante.
 
Ito ang pangmalakasang sagot ng pambato ng Pilipinas na si Maria Gigante sa question and answer portion ng Universal Woman 2024 na ginanap sa Cambodia nitong March 22.
 
Lalo pang humanga ang mga manonood pati ang mga hurado sa Cebuana beauty queen. Kaya naman siya ang kinoronahang Universal Woman ng taon.
 
First runner up ang Venezuela, sumunod ang France, Cambodia, at Dominican Republic.
 
“Parang hindi ako makapaniwala na naka-crown ako kasi ang tagal ko po itong hinintay, parang 10 years. Ang tagal. Pero everything happens in God’s perfect timing,” sabi ni Gigante.
      
Hindi naging madali ang pinagdaanan ni Maria para makamit ang tagumpay. Halos hindi rin makapaniwala ang pamilya ni Maria na nakuha nya ang korona pero ganoon na lang ang kanilang pasasalamat sa naging kapalit ng sakripisyo at pagpupursige ng dalaga.
 
“Nanginginig yung kalamnan ko. Parang, oh my God. I'm shaking. Grabe, kasi sumali na siya Binibining Cebu 2nd runner-up siya - Binibining Cebu Charity. She was actually viral but then she did not make it. And then Miss World, viral din siya but she was only up to top 11. And here, we were not expecting anything because we know how to lose already...use your voice Maria to spread kindness,” sabi ni Lindo Gigante, ama ni Maria.
 
Ayon kay Universal Woman Philippines National Director Arnold Vegafria, ilang linggo lang ang kanilang naging paghahanda ni Maria para sa kumpetisyon. Pero nagbunga ang lahat ng kanilang pagsisikap.
 
“Napakahirap nung kompetisyon lalo na sa top 5 at sa top 12 but she nailed it because I'm so confident that she has the perfect epitome of a universal woman,” pahayag ni Vegafria.
 
Para kay Maria, handa na niyang gampanan ang mga tungkulin bilang Universal Woman at patuloy na magbigay karangalan sa bansa.
 
Para sa iba pang ulat patungkol sa mga Pilipino sa iba't ibang bahagi ng mundo, panoorin at tumutok sa TFC News sa TV Patrol.

headline: Comelec: Safeguards in place against foreign interference | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA — The Commission on Elections assured the public Wednesday that systems are in place to prevent foreign interference in the 2025 midterm elections amid concerns about it raised by the National Security Council.
 On Wednesday, NSC Assistant Director General Jonathan Malaya said their office has seen foreign interference in the elections of other countries.  
In a message, Comelec Chairman George Garcia said this is precisely what they are trying to prevent in their early preparation for the polls.
“Procurement is timely. This ensures that the system is tested and all security measures are put in place. The features and the wishlists that we included in the hardware, software and transmission ensure the protection against any cyber attack. Of course the readiness of our IT personnel matters much coupled with our learnings from the past,” Garcia said. 
“Most important of all is the support of all stakeholders in the electoral process and the critical collaboration with the electorate. Vigilance is therefore crucial,” he added.

SAFEGUARDS IN VOTE COUNTING EQUIPMENT
Comelec spokesperson John Rex Laudiangco also guaranteed that the results cannot be manipulated.
“You'll find that the Automated Counting Machine and our servers are standalone the whole period of voting and counting, and connected to the Telcos only at the point of transmission, which is after the Close Voting and Printing of the first 8 Election Returns, assuring everyone that the digital results cannot be altered in any way,” Laudiango pointed out.
The machines are configured to only transmit and not receive data, according to Laudiangco.
Voters can also double check if their votes were recorded correctly through the machine’s screen.  
READ: OpenAI seeks to allay election meddling fears in blog post
Another safeguard, according to the spokesperson, are the printed election receipts which will be given to watchers and other stakeholders.
There will also be multiple Data Centers where results can be monitored. These will be dedicated for the poll body, boards of canvassers, dominant majority and minority party, accredited citizens arms, and the media.
“These measures, which will similarly be implemented in the Transmission Services and the Online Voting and Counting System (OVCS, Internet Voting), all add to the resilience and robustness of the whole Automated Elections System,” Laudiangco mentioned.
The Random Manual Audit is also in place and will “verify, audit, and account for the accuracy and transparency” of the automated elections, according to the poll body.

headline: Ilang researcher sugatan matapos paikutan ng China helicopter ang Sandy Cay 3 | ABS-CBN News
text: Kakaibang tunog ang narinig ng ilang University of the Philippine biologist na nagsasagawa ng scientific research sa Sandy Cay 3 noong Sabado. Ito'y habang pinaiikutang ng Chinese helicopter ang ilang mga kasama nilang researchers na nasa itaas ng isla. Nagpa-Patrol, Jose Carretero. TV Patrol, Miyerkules. 27 Marso 2024

headline: SILIPIN: Lagay ng mga Sandy Cay sa Pag-asa Island, Palawan | ABS-CBN News
text:
MAYNILA—Ayon kay Dr. Jonathan Anticamara ng UP Institute of Biology, sa inisyal na pagsusuri at pag-aaral, bumaba ang populasyon ng mga isda sa Sandy Cays sa Pag-asa Island. 
Overfishing ang isa sa nakikitang dahilan nito. 
Kapansin-pansin din ang corals sa Sandy Cays. Inaalam pa kung tinambak ang mga ito o dahil sa natural na pangyayari. 
Nagsagawa ng isang joint maritime resource assessment ang UP Institute of Biology, Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources (BFAR), at National Fisheries Research and Development Institute (NFRDI) sa mga Sandy Cay sa Pag-asa Island. 
Kahit matagumpay ang misyon, sinubukang haranging ng Chinese maritime militia ang team.
Inaangkin din ng China ang mga Sandy Cay. 
– Ulat ni Jose Carretero , Patrol ng Pilipino

headline: For camareros, taking care of religious images is a way of life | ABS-CBN News
text:
Camareros prepare the beautiful carrozas with the  life-size religious images which are part of the Baliwag, Bulacan Procession that will happen later at 6 pm.
MANILA — For as long as 50-year-old Ma. Victoria Tengco-Burgos can remember, she has been participating in Holy Week processions in her hometown of Baliwag, Bulacan.
It is one of the religious activities her family has always participated in thanks to her devout grandmother.
Victoria embraced that faith and today, the Tengco family is known in Baliwag as one of the families with the most number of carrozas or “karo” with various religious images. They are also called  “camareros” or people who take care of images.
For the Holy Week Procession or “Prusisyon ng mga tagpo sa buhay ng ating Panginoong Hesukristo” on Holy Wednesday, five of the 11 carrozas of the family are included.
“Siguro po ito po yung paraan ng pasasalamat din namin, ng pamilya po sa mga blessings din po na natatanggap namin. Kami po noong bata na-train na nagtutuhog ng sampaguita. ‘Di ba po kapag Holy Week ‘yung iba umaalis, out of the country, kami po talaga nandito talaga nagpu-prusisyon,” Victoria shared.  
(Maybe this is our way to thank the Lord for all the blessings that we receive. When we were young, we were trained to make “sampaguita” garlands. Others, during Holy Week, travel, go out of the country. We stay and take part in processions.”
It takes much devotion for anyone to be a camarero, especially since it costs a lot.
According to Brother Keno Enrile, head organizer of the procession, a carroza ranges from P50,000 to millions of pesos depending on the size, design, and number of images.
Despite this, Victoria says it is part of their devotion and tradition which is why they are passing it on to the younger members of their family.
“‘Yung pag-aayos po, ‘yung mula sa puso, hindi ‘yung kaya mo lang ginagawa para mapansin ka, makita,” Victoria stated. “Ine-encourage din po nito ‘yung mga kabataan sa generation naman po nila na ipagpatuloy din po nila ito.”
(This is something that’s done through the goodness of your heart, not something you do just to be noticed. We also encourage the youth to continue this.)
OLDEST AND GRANDEST HOLY WEEK PROCESSION
Baliwag’s Holy Week procession dates back to the 1800s. It is considered as the longest and grandest Holy Week procession in the country, according to Enrile.
This Holy Wednesday, 123 carrozas are participating.
Enrile calls it a “moving catechism” since they hope to teach the public about Christ’s life, sacrifices, and suffering through the procession.
“Sa gawain pong ito ay inaalala po namin mula sa misyon ni Kristo, sa Kanyang pagpapakasakit hanggang sa kamatayan. Ito rin po yung pagkakataon ng mga taga-Baliwag para mag-reunion,” Enrile remarked.
(Through this activity, we are reminded of the mission of Christ, of His sacrifice until His death. This is also a chance for Baliwag residents to get together and have a reunion.”)  
The procession is expected to last for three hours with certain roads being closed.
Father Prince Marcelo, parish vicar of the Diocesan Shrine and Parish of Saint Augustine, hopes the Lord’s words will touch Filipinos through this solemn celebration.
“The message of Jesus remains constant. Kaya natin pinapaalala, kaya tayo nagdiriwang ng ganito, to remind us that God is love. Ang Diyos ay pag-ibig. Kung hindi tayo mahal ng Diyos, hindi Niya gagawin ang lahat ng ito. Now more than ever, maraming naghihirap, maraming nangangailangan, it remains to be an invitation to us to follow the example of the Lord,” Marcelo said.
(The message of Jesus remains constant. The reason why we remind, why we celebrate, is to remind us that God is love. God is love. If He doesn’t love us, He will not do all of these. Now more than ever, many are poor, many are in need, it remains to be an invitation to us to follow the example of the Lord.)

headline: Winning Moment: Easter activities kasama si tsikiting | ABS-CBN News
text: Pagkatapos ng Mahal na Araw, dapat i-celebrate ang Easter. Narito ang ilang puwedeng gawin para ipagdiwang ang muling pagkabuhay ni Kristo. Nagpa-Patrol, Winnie Cordero. TV Patrol, Miyerkules. 27 Marso 2024

headline: Mga motorista muling hinikayat na magpakabit ng RFID | ABS-CBN News
text: Ramdam na sa mga expressway patungong northern at southern Luzon ang dagsa ng mas maraming motorista ngayong Miyerkules Santo. Muli namang hinikayat ng mga expressway operator ang mga motorista na magpakabit na ng RFID para mas mabilis ang pagdaan sa mga toll gate. Nagpa-Patrol, Johnson Manabat. TV Patrol, Miyerkules, 27 Marso 2024

headline: ​Higit 100 na life-size na imahen tampok sa Holy Week procession sa Bulacan | ABS-CBN News
text: Muling idinaos ang dinarayong Holy Week procession sa Baliwag, Bulacan kung saan tampok ang mahigit isangdaang life-size na mga imahen. Nagpa-Patrol, Victoria Tulad. TV Patrol, Miyerkules. 27 Marso 2024

headline: Miyerkules Santo: Mga uuwi sa probinsya dagsa sa Batangas Port | ABS-CBN News
text: Mahaba na ang pila ng mga sasakyan sa labas ng Batangas Port. Inanunsiyo naman ng isang shipping line na magbabawas sila ng biyahe sa ilang ruta sa Biyernes Santo. Nagpa-Patrol, Dennis Datu. TV Patrol, Miyerkules, 27 Marso 2024

headline: Duterte nagka-'gentleman's agreement' sa China tungkol sa West PH Sea: Roque | ABS-CBN News
text: Inamin ni dating presidential spokesperson Harry Roque na nagkaroon ng "gentleman's agreement" sa pagitan ni dating Pangulong Rodrigo Duterte at pamahalaan ng China kaugnay sa West Philippine Sea. Nagpa-Patrol, Zen Hernandez. TV Patrol, Miyerkules. 27 Marso 2024

headline: 3 magkakapatid huli sa human trafficking, online sexual abuse | ABS-CBN News
text:
(UPDATE) Nagpanggap na customer ang isang asset ng National Bureau Investigation para makipag-video call sa suspek na si "Maria" na nago-online show umano. 
Maya-maya, pinakita ni "Maria" sa video call ang pamangkin niyang dalawang taong gulang at ang pinsan niyang 15 taong gulang.
Ito na ang naging hudyat ng NBI Violence Against Women and Children Division (VAWCD) para pasukin ang bahay at i-rescue ang anim na mga menor de edad doon. Inaresto si Maria at ang dalawa niyang kapatid.
Natukoy sila matapos mahuli sa ibang bansa ang parokyanong bumibili ng mga litrato at video ng mga bata. 
Ayon kay Yehlen Agus, hepe ng NBI-VAWCD, "Doon sa engagement ino-offer niya ang pamangkin at pinsan sa foreigners na hubo't hubad with a fee."
Inako naman ni "Maria" ang krimen. Dati pa raw siyang nag-vi-video call sa mga "dating site," hanggang sinama na niya ang mga menor de edad na kaanak. 
"Nagisisi ako kasi alam ko sa ngayon hindi ko alam kung saan ito patungo. 'Di na ako tatanggapin ng kapatid ko at ng magulang ko. Kahit ang kapatid ko galit na galit sa akin. Ako naman talaga ang may kasalanan eh."
Paliwanag naman ng dalawang kapatid ni "Maria," alam nilang nag-vi-video show siya pero hindi raw nila alam na kinukuhanan na pati ang kanilang anak at pinsan - kahit ginagawa ito sa kanilang bahay. Dumaan din umano ang bayad sa kanilang e-wallet.
"'Yong anak ko may picture nagulat ako, two years 'yong anak ko. Paano niya na-picture-an ang anak ko? Paano nangyari 'yon? Hindi ko ginusto ang nangyari. Hindi ganyan ang pagpapalaki sa amin. Kahit labandera nanay ko hindi kami papayag sa ganyan," ayon sa kapatid ng suspect na si "Princess."
Kahirapan ang dinadahilan ni "Maria" kung bakit daw niya nagawa ang krimen. Pero ayon sa NBI, hindi ito sapat na dahilan para madamay ang mga bata. 
"Akala nila picture na hubot hubad lang. May psychological effect ito sa mga bata, may trauma. Itong mga picture na ito hindi natin alam kung hanggang kailan, hindi natin alam kung hanggang kailan ito magsi-circulate sa internet," ani Agus.
Dinala muna sa kustodiya ng Department of Social Welfare and Development ang mga bata. Kulong ang tatlo sa kasong Human Trafficking at Online Sexual Abuse. 
-- Ulat ni Niko Baua, ABS-CBN News
